method: market scan -> mispricing score -> stock-vs-LEAPS choice -> ranked picks

rows.name: MP promoted structural pick on Rare Earth Magnet Sovereignty
rows.thesis: The market may still frame MP too narrowly as a mining story even though the company is becoming a policy-backed mine-to-magnet platform tied to the most constrained layers.
why_missed: market framing appears stale relative to the structural role
the market may still anchor on an outdated business description
invalidations: MP fails to scale beyond a partial magnet-manufacturing foothold
non-China capacity expands broadly enough to collapse scarcity value
policy support does not translate into durable economics

parse_evidence_summary.statement: The market may still frame MP too narrowly as a mining story even though the company is becoming a policy-backed mine-to-magnet platform tied to the most constrained layers.

mispricing.candidate_name: MP promoted structural pick on Rare Earth Magnet Sovereignty
mispricing.explanation: MP promoted structural pick on Rare Earth Magnet Sovereignty scores 61.2/100 (moderate) on mispricing plausibility. The strongest weighted drivers are recognition gap, hiddenness, duration mismatch. The least supportive signals are crowding inverse, valuation nonlinearity. This screen is a structural ranking aid, not live options pricing.

options_fit.candidate_name: MP promoted structural pick on Rare Earth Magnet Sovereignty
options_fit.explanation: MP promoted structural pick on Rare Earth Magnet Sovereignty scores 73.4/100 (high) on options-expression fit. The strongest weighted drivers are tenor alignment, vol expansion potential, downside definedness. The least supportive signals are catalyst timing specificity, convexity need. This screen helps decide whether options are a sensible expression path.

rows.name: MP anchor promoted structural pick on Robotics Supply Chain
rows.thesis: The market may be underweighting Western rare-earth processing and magnet-material names as cleaner robotics-actuation exposure than downstream robot narratives.
why_missed: market framing appears stale relative to the structural role
invalidations: robotic actuation shifts away from permanent magnet motor architectures
China concentration in processing and finished magnets falls faster than expected
MP, NEO, and UUUU fail to capture economics from the relevant processing or magnet layers

parse_evidence_summary.statement: The market may be underweighting Western rare-earth processing and magnet-material names as cleaner robotics-actuation exposure than downstream robot narratives.

theme_equity_decomposition.candidate_summary: MP is treated as a anchor expression for robotics supply chain through Sintered NdFeB Magnet Manufacturing, Neodymium Processing.

rows.name: SIVE promoted structural pick on CPO / Silicon Photonics Laser Supply
rows.thesis: Independent partner and ecosystem evidence suggests the market may still treat Sivers as a messy small Nordic photonics company even though it sits upstream of external-light-source architectures that are technically and commercially moving forward.
why_missed: market framing appears stale relative to the structural role
upstream supplier role is not obvious from headline narratives
invalidations: the partner confirmations fail to translate into meaningful shipped volume
external light source architecture adoption stalls or slips materially
Sivers remains unable to scale competitively despite the WIN path

parse_evidence_summary.statement: Independent partner and ecosystem evidence suggests the market may still treat Sivers as a messy small Nordic photonics company even though it sits upstream of external-light-source architectures that are technically and commercially moving forward.
